Paper_Metadata.Paper_Title: Achieving superior high-temperature mechanical properties in Al-Cu-Li-Sc-Zr alloy with nano-scale microstructure via laser additive manufacturing

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition from gas-atomized powder: Al-3.89Cu-1.22Li-0.98Sc-0.43Zr (wt%)

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy powder with composition Al-3.89Cu-1.22Li-0.98Sc-0.43Zr (wt%) was used as feed-stock for LPBF process using a LiM-X260A LPBF machine. Samples were fabricated under laser power of 300 W, scanning velocity of 1200 mm/s, hatch spacing of 0.1 mm, and layer thickness of 40 um. Samples in as-fabricated (AF) condition were tested without additional heat treatment.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF fabricated Al-Cu-Li-Sc-Zr alloy using LiM-X260A; laser power 300 W, scanning speed 1200 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, layer thickness 40 um; as-fabricated (AF) condition.

Microstructure_Text.original: Nearly random distribution of grain orientations in AF sample, supported by weak texture with maximum texture intensity of 2.79. IPF maps exhibited fine equiaxed grains and coarse columnar grains. Average sizes: fine equiaxed grains 1.49 um, coarse columnar grains 1.84 um. TKD analysis identified Al2Cu and AlCuSc phases predominantly located at grain boundaries. Stacking of Cu-rich and Sc-rich precipitates along equiaxed grain boundaries. Precipitates effectively impeded grain coarsening by localizing at grain boundaries. Average precipitate size 116+-42 nm in equiaxed grain area, 117+-32 nm in coarse grain area, 42+-13 nm within coarse grains (interdendritic). No discernible Zr segregation observed; Zr was supersaturated in Al matrix. Nanoscale structure with equiaxed grains as small as a few hundred nanometers.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Nearly random grain orientation with weak texture (intensity 2.79); fine equiaxed grains (~1.49 um) and coarse columnar grains (~1.84 um); Al2Cu and AlCuSc precipitates at grain boundaries (116-117 nm); smaller precipitates within grains (42 nm); no significant grain coarsening in AF state.

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature (RT); strain rate not specified; ASTM B557M-10 standard; Zwick/Roell Z020 Universal Materials Tester
Properties_Info.Test_Specimen: ASTM B557M-10; gauge section 32 mm length, 6 mm width, 3 mm thickness
Properties_Info.Note: As-fabricated (AF) condition; room temperature test from related study Qi et al. 2021

Properties_Info.Note: As-fabricated (AF) condition; approximately 83% of room-temperature yield strength

Composition_Info.Note: Nominal composition from gas-atomized powder: Al-3.89Cu-1.22Li-0.98Sc-0.43Zr (wt%); heat-treated (HT) condition after LPBF fabrication

Process_Text.original: Gas-atomized alloy powder with composition Al-3.89Cu-1.22Li-0.98Sc-0.43Zr (wt%) was used as feed-stock for LPBF process using a LiM-X260A LPBF machine. Samples were fabricated under laser power of 300 W, scanning velocity of 1200 mm/s, hatch spacing of 0.1 mm, and layer thickness of 40 um. Heat-treated (HT) samples underwent gradual heating at a rate of 10 °C/min from room temperature to 300 °C inside a KSL-1400X muffle furnace, held at 300 °C for 1 h, then cooled to ambient temperature in air.
Process_Text.simplified: LPBF fabricated Al-Cu-Li-Sc-Zr alloy using LiM-X260A; laser power 300 W, scanning speed 1200 mm/s, hatch spacing 0.1 mm, layer thickness 40 um; subsequently heat-treated (HT) at 300 °C for 1 h with 10 °C/min heating rate, air cooled.

Process_Info.Equipment: LiM-X260A LPBF machine; KSL-1400X muffle furnace

Microstructure_Text.original: Nearly random distribution of grain orientations in HT sample, supported by weak texture with maximum texture intensity of 1.83. IPF maps exhibited fine equiaxed grains and coarse columnar grains. Average sizes: fine equiaxed grains 1.53 um, coarse columnar grains 1.76 um. No significant grain coarsening after heat treatment at elevated temperatures. Increased number of precipitates in both equiaxed and columnar grain areas. Fine precipitates within columnar grains coalesced and grew to 72+-21 nm (71% growth compared to AF). Precipitation particles nearly continuously stacked along grain boundaries (125+-39 nm for equiaxed grains, 123+-37 nm for coarse grains). Nano-sized precipitates encircling nano-scale equiaxed grains gave rise to distinctive ring-shaped structure at nanoscale; strip-shaped structure along columnar grain boundaries. These structures stabilized precipitate pinning effect against grain boundary migration.
Microstructure_Text.simplified: Nearly random grain orientation with weak texture (intensity 1.83); fine equiaxed grains (~1.53 um) and coarse columnar grains (~1.76 um); no significant grain coarsening after HT; increased precipitates with nearly continuous stacking at grain boundaries (123-125 nm); fine precipitates within grains grew to 72 nm (71% increase from AF).

Properties_Info.Test_Condition: at room temperature (RT); strain rate not specified; ASTM B557M-10 standard; Zwick/Roell Z020 Universal Materials Tester
